subject: Math
domain: Number and Operations in Base Ten

lesson.story_mission: Help the Number Gatekeepers place the correct comparison symbol so each bridge opens safely.
lesson.mini_lesson: Compare three-digit numbers from left to right by place value. First compare hundreds. If hundreds are equal, compare tens. If tens are equal, compare ones. Use > for greater than, < for less than, and = for equal to.
lesson.objective: Compare three-digit numbers using hundreds, tens, ones, and the symbols >, <, and =.
lesson.concept_explanation: The first place where the digits differ decides which number is greater. If every place matches, the numbers are equal.
lesson.common_misconception: A child may reverse the symbol or compare only the first digit they notice instead of using place value.
lesson.real_world_connection: Comparing numbers helps choose higher scores, larger page numbers, and bigger collections.
lesson.lesson_summary: Compare hundreds, then tens, then ones, and select the symbol that makes the statement true.

worked_examples.text: 642 > 542 because 6 hundreds is greater than 5 hundreds.
381 < 392 because the hundreds are equal, but 8 tens is less than 9 tens.
515 = 515 because every digit is the same.

hint_ladder: If hundreds tie, compare tens. If tens tie, compare ones.
Choose the symbol that points to the smaller number.

hints: Focus on 345 and 268; start at the greatest place and move right.
Compare 345 with 268 at hundreds place, then choose the symbol that makes the statement true.
Check your choice by reading the complete comparison aloud: 345 is ___ 268.

feedback.incorrect: Try again. Compare 345 and 268 from the greatest place to the ones place.

guided_practice.explanation: 345 is greater than 268 because the hundreds digits are the first digits that differ: 3 is greater than 2.
guided_practice.visual_description: comparison comparing 345 and 268. 345 is greater than 268.

hints: Focus on 456 and 432; start at the greatest place and move right.
Compare 456 with 432 at tens place, then choose the symbol that makes the statement true.
Check your choice by reading the complete comparison aloud: 456 is ___ 432.

feedback.incorrect: Try again. Compare 456 and 432 from the greatest place to the ones place.

guided_practice.explanation: 456 is greater than 432 because the tens digits are the first digits that differ: 5 is greater than 3.
guided_practice.visual_description: place value chart comparing 456 and 432. 456 is greater than 432.

misconception_bank.compares_left_to_right_incorrectly: Compares a visible digit without using the place-value order.
misconception_bank.symbol_reversal: Chooses the symbol pointing the wrong way.
misconception_bank.place_value_compare_error: Does not move from hundreds to tens to ones correctly.
misconception_bank.equal_confusion: Does not recognize equality when both numbers match.

game_theme.title: Comparison Gatekeepers
game_theme.mission_intro: Open each bridge by comparing three-digit numbers.

story.text: Listen to this page. You are starting a math mission about Compare Three-Digit Numbers. Look at the story and think about what the characters need to solve. Math missions are easier when you name the numbers and the model first. Press Start Mission when you are ready.
story.label: Read This Page

lesson.text: This lesson teaches Compare Three-Digit Numbers. Look at the comparison and connect each part to the math words. Use the vocabulary on the page to explain your thinking. Remember to check place value, units, or symbols before you answer. Press Next to continue.
lesson.label: Read This Page

watch.text: This page lets you watch a worked example for Compare Three-Digit Numbers. Follow the model one step at a time. Say what each number means before you look at the final answer. If a symbol appears, read it in words like plus, minus, equals, greater than, or less than. Press Next to continue.
watch.label: Read This Page

demo.text: This demo helps you try Compare Three-Digit Numbers with support. Build or study the model first, then connect it to the question. A good strategy is to explain the answer in math words before choosing. Press Next when you are ready for practice.
demo.label: Read This Page

practice.text: This is practice. Use the visual model to help you think about Compare Three-Digit Numbers. Press Read Question if you want the exact question read aloud. Check your strategy before you choose or write an answer. When you are ready, answer the question and keep going.
practice.label: Read This Page

challenge.text: This is a challenge. Use the visual model to help you think about Compare Three-Digit Numbers. Press Read Question if you want the exact question read aloud. Check your strategy before you choose or write an answer. When you are ready, answer the question and keep going.
challenge.label: Read This Page

checkpoint.text: This is your checkpoint. Use the visual model to help you think about Compare Three-Digit Numbers. Press Read Question if you want the exact question read aloud. Check your strategy before you choose or write an answer. When you are ready, answer the question and keep going.
checkpoint.label: Read This Page

badge.text: This badge page celebrates your mission work on Compare Three-Digit Numbers. Look at your stars and remember the strategy you used. Mistakes help show what to practice next. Save your growth data to continue.
badge.label: Read This Page

profile.text: This profile page shows your growth for Compare Three-Digit Numbers. Look at attempts, correct answers, accuracy, and hints used. Use the recommendation to decide what to practice next. You can continue to Skill Practice or replay the mission.
profile.label: Read This Page
